commit.message:
Add TECHNICAL_REFERENCE.md for papyrus_find_signature

Comprehensive technical documentation covering:
- Two-phase architecture (template scoring + AC post-validation)
- Database relationships and MINOR_VERSION / STRUCTURE_DEF_ID semantics
- Scoring algorithm: all weights, thresholds, partial credit tables
- Phase 1 template character-class mask explained
- Phase 2 Aho-Corasick automaton (DFA vs NFA mode)
- Classification logic (SIMPLIFIED / COMPLEX / NO_SIGNATURE / NO_SAMPLES)
- All CLI parameters with usage examples
- Output file formats (CSV + JSON column reference)
- Key C++ data structures
- Tuning guide: weights, thresholds, speed vs coverage trade-offs
- Troubleshooting guide for common failure modes
- Full bug history (7 bugs fixed during initial development)
- Build instructions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

migration failed silently under PgBouncer transaction mode.

Merge: Architecture search results + experiment log updates

Shallow/wide models dominate on ANE in fixed time budgets.
Best config: 4L, dim=1024, 95M params → loss 5.357 in 2 min
vs SmolLM2-360M 362M params → loss 5.682 in 5 min.

commit.message:
Architecture search results: shallow/wide beats deep/narrow on ANE

4L/dim=1024 (95M params) achieves loss 5.357 in 2 min, beating
SmolLM2-360M (362M params) which gets 5.682 in 5 min.

Key finding: fewer layers = less ANE overhead per step = more
optimizer steps in fixed time = better loss. Validates autoresearch
insight that step count matters more than model size.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix shiv install shimmer auth: add GH_TOKEN to step

shiv uses gh to clone private repos. The Install shimmer step
was missing GH_TOKEN, causing all scheduled agent jobs to fail
since the shiv-based workflow landed (efe28f2 in fold).

Set step-level (not job-level) to avoid exposing the PAT to the
curl-piped shiv installer in the preceding step.

commit.message:
Force NCCL_DEBUG=INFO — system module sets VERSION which is insufficient

The brics/aws-ofi-nccl module sets NCCL_DEBUG=VERSION in the environment,
which the previous ${NCCL_DEBUG:-INFO} default couldn't override. VERSION
produces too little I/O to work around the OFI CXI race condition.

commit.message:
feat: drag & drop files into terminal panes + reliable window drag (#97)

* feat: drag & drop files into terminal panes

- Add `copy_file_to_dir` Tauri command (Rust) for copying files to a target directory
- Add shared `DragOverlay` component for visual drop feedback
- Add `useFileDrop` hook for reusable drag & drop logic
- Wire terminal pane to accept file drops and copy to shell CWD
- Add spec document: specs/drag-drop-files-into-panes.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: validate target_dir is a directory in copy_file_to_dir

Addresses reagentx review: exists() alone doesn't guard against
target_dir being a file path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: recursive dir copy + stuck drag overlay for dnd

- file_ops.rs: add copy_recursive() to handle files AND directories;
  strip is_file() guard; normalize OSC-7 forward-slash CWD to OS
  separator before Path construction on Windows
- useFileDrop.ts: replace fragile bounds-check with enter/leave counter;
  document dragend/drop listeners as safety net for drags that exit
  the window without dropping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: pin header controls right + tabs left under chrome zoom

window-header width: 100vw with CSS zoom causes inner coordinate space
to shrink by 1/zoom, pushing flex children toward center.
Fix: width: calc(100vw / var(--zoomfactor, 1)) so the header renders
at exactly 100vw after zoom is applied.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: seed cmd:cwd on shell spawn + fix MSYS2 path handling for dnd

- Thread Arc<WaveStore> into ShellController so cmd:cwd can be written
  immediately after spawn, before the first OSC 7 fires. Fixes
  "Working directory not detected" for fresh terminals and shells
  without integration (cmd.exe).
- normalize_path_for_platform() in file_ops.rs now handles both
  MSYS2-style /c/foo paths and forward-slash Windows C:/foo paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: eliminate window drag dead spots — data-tauri-drag-region only

Replace dual JS startDragging() + data-tauri-drag-region mechanism with
data-tauri-drag-region alone. The two mechanisms raced each other causing
non-deterministic dead spots: Tauri native drag fired synchronously at OS
level while async startDragging() IPC calls collided with it, and event
bubbling from WindowDrag to window-header called startDragging() twice per
click. Also add data-tauri-drag-region to the outer window-header div so the
full empty header area is covered (not just the 10px WindowDrag.left strip).

Linux unaffected: dragProps returns {} on Linux, GTK/drag.rs handles natively.
macOS/Windows: data-tauri-drag-region is synchronous, reliable, and Tauri
correctly honours data-tauri-drag-region="false" on TabBar and window controls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: bump version to 0.31.100

* fix: pass wstore arg to resync_controller in websocket handler

resync_controller gained a 7th wstore parameter; the call site in
websocket.rs was missing it, causing a compile error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: congress scraper 0 trades — 3 root causes fixed

1. Unique constraint missing on existing NAS DB: ON CONFLICT was silently
   failing. Fixed by adding DO $$ ALTER TABLE ADD CONSTRAINT IF NOT EXISTS $$
   in initDB() — runs on every startup, safe to re-run.

2. Quiverquant API intermittent 500s: Added 3-attempt retry with 2s/4s backoff.
   API works reliably on retry.

3. Silent upsert errors: upsertTrade errors now logged to console. Seed
   threshold changed from count=0 to count<10 to catch partial failures.

Also: 5s startup delay before initial scrape (DB/Redis fully ready).
ON CONFLICT now uses named constraint (congress_trades_unique_key).

commit.message:
Speed fix: pass page_limit to extractor as 1-N range instead of ALL

Previously the external tool was called with ALL pages even though
only the first page_limit pages were ever scored. For large RPT files
(hundreds/thousands of pages) this caused the tool to hang while
extracting all pages unnecessarily.

Fix: build a page range string "1-{page_limit}" and pass it to
papyrus_rpt_search_page_extract.exe instead of ALL. When page_limit=0
the old ALL behaviour is preserved (backward compatible).

With the default --page-limit 1 this means only page 1 is extracted
per instance — typically 10-100x faster on large files.
Use --page-limit 2 to extract the first 2 pages if needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable Codex auto-resume by default — attach to active threads

The codex app-server subprocess only forwards approval requests for
threads it has attached to via thread/resume.  Auto-resume was gated
behind DECKY_CODEX_AUTO_RESUME=1, so the subprocess sat idle and never
received approval events.  Now auto-resume defaults to on, using
process.cwd() for thread selection.  Set DECKY_CODEX_AUTO_RESUME=0
to disable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add --literal-only flag to papyrus_find_signature

Adds a new matching mode that scores only on literal anchor characters
(e.g. ':', '/', '-', '.') and ignores 'A'/'9'/space wildcard positions.

Rationale: literal anchors define the structural skeleton of a header
line. Scoring only on anchors is more discriminating — two headers
'REPORT DATE: 1/12/25' vs 'STATEMENT DATE: 2/01/25' match very
differently on their ':' and '/' positions even though their A/9
positions are indistinguishable. Useful when the default weighted
scoring produces too many borderline matches.

Changes:
- Config.literal_only field (--literal-only CLI flag)
- scoreTemplateMatch(tmpl, line, literal_only): A/9/space positions
  do continue (zero weight) when literal_only=true
- matchSignatureAgainstPage(..., literal_only): propagates to scorer
- Main loop passes cfg.literal_only
- printUsage / parseArgs updated

Also adds --keep-txt / --keep-txt-dir to printUsage help text
(implementation was already in previous commit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Sentry alerting for Inngest and email failures

Emails were failing silently because all email functions swallowed
errors in try-catch blocks, preventing Inngest retries from triggering.
Additionally, no alerting existed for permanent failures.

Changes:
- Remove silent try-catch from Inngest-called email functions so errors
  propagate and retries work correctly
- Add Sentry.captureException to directly-called email functions
  (password reset, email verification) that still need try-catch
- Add Inngest Sentry middleware to capture errors from all function runs
- Add onFailure handlers to all Inngest functions for permanent failure
  alerting
- Wrap chat email polling loop with Sentry error reporting so one
  failure doesn't block remaining emails
- Fix email-notifications tests to assert on inngest.send() instead of
  the mocked email function

- sharp_book_lean_over (41.7% HR) → SHADOW + removed from weights/rescue
- over_streak_reversion_under (51.6% HR) → removed from registry

New shadow signals (accumulating BB data):
- hot_3pt_under: 62.5% HR (N=670), cold_3pt_over: 60.2% (N=123),
  line_drifted_down_under: 59.8% (N=336)

New observation filters:
- cold_fg_under_obs (38.5% HR), cold_3pt_under_obs (45.6%),
  over_line_rose_heavy_obs (38.9%)

BettingPros line movement query added to supplemental_data + per_model_pipeline.
Shooting stats (fg/3pt) now in pred dict. Algorithm v462_simulator_validated.
236 tests passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

arod analytics: fix XSS in live activity JS by using textContent

Replace innerHTML string concatenation with createElement/textContent
for all user-controlled values (path, method, timestamp, etc.) in the
live activity feed refresh. Malicious request paths logged to the DB
can no longer inject HTML/JS into the dashboard.

commit.message:
feat: include all app domains in ACME TLS cert (SAN list)

At startup, collect all configured domains from the database
(domain, domains JSON array, auto_subdomain) and include them
as Subject Alternative Names in the Let's Encrypt certificate.

This enables HTTPS for app subdomains like nixpacks-test.rivetr.site
without needing wildcard DNS-01 challenges.

traefik.me and sslip.io auto-domains are excluded from the SAN list
as those use external DNS and don't need LE certs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite lesson-31 style block; unify all month shimmer angles to 135deg

lesson-31:
- Completely rewrote the <style> block from scratch with clean, minimal CSS
- Root cause of mobile content disappearing: inputs inside week-planner have
  browser-enforced minimum widths that cannot be suppressed inside an
  overflow:hidden parent (scroll-container) — the planner grid forced layout
  wider than the viewport, clipping everything below it
- Fix: hide .planner-area on mobile via display:none (it requires desktop space)
- Grade tracker: flex-wrap + height:auto so D-I-L-I-G dots wrap to two rows
- Study types: 2-column grid on mobile
- Strategy cards: single column on mobile
- All grid minmax values tightened for mobile-friendliness

catalogue:
- Unified all 12 month header gradients to exactly 135deg
  (previously 110-150deg per month, causing the shimmer band to slant
  differently on each card and look inconsistent)
- All month colours and 5-stop metallic structure preserved

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
